vulnerability.title: Ensure gnutls-utils is installed
vulnerability.description: The gnutls-utils package can be installed with the following command: $ sudo dnf install gnutls-utils
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: if ! rpm -q --quiet "gnutls-utils" ; then dnf install -y "gnutls-utils" fi

triage_decision.reason: The gnutls-utils package provides essential TLS utilities; without it, systems may lack up-to-date TLS support, increasing risk of TLS-related vulnerabilities.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: networking affected; Missing package leads to outdated TLS support; Potential vulnerability to TLS attacks

reasoning_messages.reasoning:
We need to produce JSON with plan_description and commands array. The task: Remediation is to ensure gnutls-utils is installed. So we need to run dnf install gnutls-utils (since Rocky Linux uses dnf). Possibly need to check if installed first? But the remediation script says: if ! rpm -q --quiet "gnutls-utils" ; then dnf install -y "gnutls-utils" fi. That's a shell snippet. We need to translate into a command using run_cmd. Probably just one command: dnf install -y gnutls-utils. Could also do check then install but run_cmd only allows one command. Could we embed a conditional? Not easily. Perhaps we can just run the install command; if already installed, dnf will say nothing to do and exit 0. That's acceptable.

Thus plan_description: "Ensure gnutls-utils package is installed on Rocky Linux 10 by installing it via dnf if not already present."

Commands: [ { "command": "dnf install -y gnutls-utils" } ]

Return JSON.
